title: Confiscation of vehicle in excise cases - how to get it released
p1:
Your vehicle was seized in an excise case… does that mean you lose it forever?

Many people assume that once a vehicle is confiscated, there is no way to get it back. But the law provides remedies that many vehicle owners are unaware of.
p2: A common misconception is that the vehicle’s owner automatically becomes guilty when the vehicle is seized. In reality, seizure and confiscation are different stages. A vehicle may be taken temporarily during investigation, but the owner can challenge its continued retention and seek release through the proper legal process.
p3:
The key is acting quickly and presenting the right facts, such as proving ownership, explaining lack of involvement, and following the procedure available under excise laws. One surprising point: even an innocent owner may need to actively approach the authorities or court to protect their property rights.

Knowing your rights early can prevent unnecessary loss and delay.
conclusion: Have you ever seen a vehicle being seized in an excise case? Do you think owners should get automatic protection if they were not involved?
